blocks.content: Not a text. A call. His phone buzzed on his nightstand at 4:47 on a Tuesday and it was her name on the screen and he picked up before the second ring.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Early action. I just — I got the email. I'm looking at it right now.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I think so. I don't know. It's a lot.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I called you first. Before Savannah. I don't know why I'm telling you that.
Quiet on the line. Not uncomfortable. The kind of quiet they'd always been able to sit in.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I'm on the porch. It's cold.

He held the phone and listened to the quiet from her end, and she held hers, and neither of them said the thing that was sitting right there between the silence. They weren't ready yet. But they were closer than they'd ever been.
